claim: The proposal protocol shall include a ProposedConflict kind (kind='Conflict') that materializes a new Conflict node in the active domain's graph via `hotam apply-proposal`.
why: Closes recorded spec-tool drift: a DECIDED rationale already promised 'ProposedConflict' while only transitions existed, leaving surfaced tensions without a mechanical creation path (the loop could move conflicts but never materialize one). ProposedConflict (internal/proposal/types.go) is a real proposal kind: ProposedConflict.mutate (internal/proposal/mutate.go) computes the id via ontology.ConflictIdentity(axis, context), never caller-supplied (R-stable-conflict-identity); validate() (internal/proposal/validate.go) requires the axis to already exist in the graph's axes (R-axis-controlled-vocab; admitting a new axis is a separate act), requires >= 2 distinct member requirement ids (R-conflict-min-two-members), and refuses a steward who owns any member (R-steward-distinct-from-owners); lifecycle always starts DETECTED (or a caller-supplied initial_lifecycle, which must carry a decided_by if DECIDED). Applied via `hotam apply-proposal` (cmd/hotam/apply_proposal.go) or `hotam land`. Enforced by TestApply_Conflict_Create (internal/proposal/proposal_test.go). Extends R-active-loop-protocol's floor exactly as EntityType and OperatorBudget did. settled_at RESTORED 2026-07-13 from git HEAD 9af0176 (pre-corruption value) after a mutate.go bug this session silently reset it on content-only updates; see task #84.
